assertions.description: INCORRECT: The schematic has two power:PWR_FLAG symbols: one connected to the GND net (at 246.38,38.1 via wire to junction at 246.38,36.83) and one to the +3V3 net (at 246.38,25.4 via wire to junction at 246.38,26.67). The analyzer lists both PWR_FLAG symbols under a separate 'PWR_FLAG' power net entry in power_symbols rather than resolving them to their connected rails, then incorrectly warns that GND and +3V3 each lack a power_out driver or PWR_FLAG. Both warnings are false positives.

D1 is a Zener diode (BZV55C-5.6V, SMA package) with its cathode on the +3V3 rail and anode on GND. This is a standard overvoltage/reverse-polarity protection clamp for the power supply — especially relevant because the MAX4734 is rated for 1.6V–3.6V supply and D1 clamps overvoltage at 5.6V. The signal_analysis.protection_devices list is empty; D1 should be identified as a power supply protection device.
